summary: A solid matrix recording captures an energetic Alpine Valley show highlighted by stellar Jack Straw, Minglewood Blues and the Sugar Magnolia > Scarlet > Man Smart > Eyes sequence.
review: The July‑18‑1989 Alpine Valley performance is widely praised for its tight first set and a second‑set that bookends with Sugar Magnolia and Sunshine Daydream, delivering standout moments like Jack Straw, Minglewood Blues, Friend of the Devil and a fiery Sugar Magnolia > Scarlet > Man Smart > Eyes medley. The Brantley‑28474 matrix source provides clear, well‑balanced audio with no notable dropouts, making the show both musically and technically enjoyable.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews are overwhelmingly positive about the performance and note a clean matrix recording; weighting the strong musical moments (70%) with good sound quality (30%) yields a rating in the low‑4‑star range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Brantley‑28474 matrix source is praised for clarity and balance; no significant dropouts or muffling reported.

standout_songs: Sugar Magnolia > Scarlet > Man Smart > Eyes
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds strong momentum with a solid Touch opener and powerful Jack‑related jams; second set is playful, bookended by Sugar Magnolia and Sunshine Daydream, featuring an inventive medley that keeps the energy high.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Guided the Space segue into China Doll, showcasing his improvisational instincts.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass approach on the second set is highlighted as especially strong.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob (Bobby) wanted to move into Terrapin after Space but was redirected; no explicit praise noted.
